# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 2

This is turn 2. Turns 1 through 1 are consolidated in `turns_history__q2_refinement.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__q2_refinement.md` or `current_manuscript.tex` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 2.

## Turn 2 — the repaired normalization reduces to one principal-unit automorphism

Turn 1 showed that the missing hypothesis is the prescribed canonical orientation
\[
\chi(\bar A)=-1,\qquad \chi(\bar S)=1,\qquad
\chi(\bar Y)=\eta:=(-3)^{-1}.
\]
That counterexample does not affect the candidate, but it invalidates Proposition 3.4 as presently stated. 

Put
\[
D_0=\langle A,S,Y\mid A^2S^4[S,Y]=1\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}-2},
\qquad
t=\bar A+2\bar S.
\]
Then
\[
B=D_0^{\mathrm{ab}}
 \simeq C_2t\oplus\mathbf Z_2\bar S\oplus\mathbf Z_2\bar Y,
\]
and
\[
\chi(t)=-1,\qquad \chi(\bar S)=1,\qquad \chi(\bar Y)=\eta.
\]

Because \(\eta\equiv5\pmod 8\), it topologically generates \(1+4\mathbf Z_2\). Hence
\[
\ker(\chi:B\to\mathbf Z_2^\times)=\mathbf Z_2\bar S.
\]
It follows immediately that every orientation-preserving automorphism of \(B\) has the unique form
\[
\alpha_{u,b}:
\quad
t\longmapsto t,\qquad
\bar S\longmapsto u\bar S,\qquad
\bar Y\longmapsto\bar Y+b\bar S,
\tag{1}
\]
where \(u\in\mathbf Z_2^\times\) and \(b\in\mathbf Z_2\). In the original basis,
\[
\bar A\longmapsto
\bar A+2(1-u)\bar S,\qquad
\bar S\longmapsto u\bar S,\qquad
\bar Y\longmapsto\bar Y+b\bar S.
\tag{2}
\]

Thus the repaired Proposition 3.4 is equivalent to lifting these triangular automorphisms from \(B\) to \(D_0\).

### The entire shear direction lifts explicitly

For every \(b\in\mathbf Z_2\), define
\[
\Theta_b(A)=A^{S^b},\qquad
\Theta_b(S)=S,\qquad
\Theta_b(Y)=YS^b.
\]
These are free pro-\(2\) automorphisms, with
\(\Theta_b^{-1}=\Theta_{-b}\). Moreover,
\[
[S,YS^b]=[S,Y]^{S^b},
\]
so
\[
\Theta_b\!\left(A^2S^4[S,Y]\right)
 =(A^2)^{S^b}S^4[S,Y]^{S^b}
 =\left(A^2S^4[S,Y]\right)^{S^b}.
\]
Hence \(\Theta_b\) descends to an automorphism of \(D_0\), inducing
\[
\alpha_{1,b}.
\tag{3}
\]

So the \(b\)-parameter is completely harmless; it is not part of the remaining gap.

### The sign unit also lifts explicitly

The relation can be rewritten as
\[
Y^{-1}SY=T,\qquad T:=S^{-3}A^{-2}.
\tag{4}
\]
Define
\[
\iota(S)=S^{-1},\qquad
\iota(A)=S^{-3}A^{-1}S^3,\qquad
\iota(Y)=Y.
\]
This is an involutive free pro-\(2\) automorphism. Furthermore,
\[
\iota(T)=T^{-1},
\]
while
\[
Y^{-1}S^{-1}Y=(Y^{-1}SY)^{-1}.
\]
Therefore \(\iota\) preserves the HNN relation (4) and descends to \(D_0\).

On \(B\),
\[
t\longmapsto t,\qquad
\bar S\longmapsto-\bar S,\qquad
\bar Y\longmapsto\bar Y,
\]
so \(\iota\) induces
\[
\alpha_{-1,0}.
\tag{5}
\]

### Only one automorphism remains

Since
\[
\mathbf Z_2^\times=\{\pm1\}\times(1+4\mathbf Z_2)
\]
and \(5\) topologically generates \(1+4\mathbf Z_2\), equations (3) and (5) show that it is enough to construct a single automorphism
\[
\Psi_5\in\operatorname{Aut}(D_0)
\]
whose action on \(B\) is
\[
t\mapsto t,\qquad
\bar S\mapsto5\bar S,\qquad
\bar Y\mapsto\bar Y.
\tag{6}
\]
Indeed, the image of
\(\operatorname{Aut}(D_0)\to\operatorname{Aut}(B)\) is closed: \(D_0\) is finitely generated profinite, so its automorphism group is compact in the congruence topology. Powers of \(\Psi_5\), together with \(\iota\), therefore supply every unit \(u\), and the \(\Theta_b\) supply every shear.

This is not merely sufficient. It is essentially necessary. Let
\[
\psi(A)=A^5,\qquad \psi(S)=S^5,\qquad \psi(Y)=Y.
\]
This is a free pro-\(2\) automorphism and induces \(\alpha_{5,0}\) on \(B\). The relator
\[
r=\bigl(\psi^{-1}(r_0)\bigr)^5
\]
has:

- the same closed normal closure as \(\psi^{-1}(r_0)\);
- free abelianization \(2\bar A+4\bar S\);
- the same degree-two initial form as \(r_0\);
- the prescribed canonical orientation.

Consequently, an orientation-corrected version of Proposition 3.4 applied to this \(r\) would produce an automorphism of \(D_0\) inducing \(\alpha_{5,0}\). Hence the repaired proposition and the principal-unit lift (6) are equivalent after the explicit shear and sign constructions above.

### Rank-two reformulation of the last gap

Use the manuscript’s Nielsen variables
\[
\sigma=S,\qquad x=S^{-2}A^{-1},\qquad y=Y.
\]
Then
\[
D_0\simeq
\left\langle\sigma,x,y\ \middle|\
x^{\sigma^2}x[y,\sigma]=1
\right\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}-2},
\]
with
\[
\nu(\sigma)=1,\qquad \nu(x)=\nu(y)=0,
\]
exactly the marked maximal pro-\(2\) boundary required later in the manuscript. 

The relation is equivalently
\[
y^{-1}\sigma y=g,
\qquad
g:=x^\sigma\sigma x.
\tag{7}
\]
Thus \(D_0\) is the pro-\(2\) HNN extension of the free rank-two group
\[
E=\langle\sigma,x\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}-2}
\]
identifying \(\langle\sigma\rangle\) with \(\langle g\rangle\). Both \(\sigma\) and \(g\) are primitive.

The remaining \(\Psi_5\) problem is therefore reduced to the following uniform rank-two statement:

> There exists \(\theta_5\in\operatorname{Aut}(E)\) carrying the conjugacy classes of \(\sigma\) and \(g\) to those of \(\sigma^5\) and \(g^5\), respectively.

More generally, if
\[
\theta_u(\sigma)=c^{-1}\sigma^u c,\qquad
\theta_u(g)=d^{-1}g^u d,
\]
then it extends across (7) by
\[
y\longmapsto c^{-1}yd.
\]
So no further rank-three Demushkin correction is needed: the unresolved content is a two-generator, two-procyclic-subgroup relative power automorphism.

There is also no linear obstruction. On the abelianized normal kernel, writing \(T\) for conjugation by \(\sigma\), scaling \(\sigma\) by \(u\) replaces
\[
T-1
\quad\text{by}\quad
T^u-1=(T-1)N_u(T),
\]
where
\[
N_u(T)=\frac{T^u-1}{T-1}
\]
has augmentation \(u\in\mathbf Z_2^\times\), hence is a unit in
\(\mathbf Z_2[[T-1]]\). For \(u=5\), with \(T=1+Z\),
\[
N_5(T)\equiv1+Z^3+Z^4\pmod2.
\]
Thus the first correction begins in weight \(4\); equivalently, the first possible Zassenhaus discrepancy is cancelled by a correction with initial term
\[
\operatorname{ad}(\sigma)^3(y).
\]
The obstruction, if any, is consequently genuinely nonabelian—not an abelianization, Alexander-module, or first-graded-piece obstruction.

The manuscript currently uses Proposition 3.4 precisely to retain the integral unramified marking, rather than merely the mod-\(2\) cup form.  The reduction above isolates its exact remaining content much more sharply.

ESTABLISHED — After adding the prescribed canonical orientation, every possible marking error is \(\alpha_{u,b}\); all shears \(b\) and the sign \(u=-1\) lift by explicit automorphisms. The full repaired normalization is equivalent to one principal-unit lift, for example \(u=5\), and this reduces further to a rank-two relative power automorphism of the HNN base \(\langle\sigma,x\rangle\).

GAP — Construct, or obstruct, an automorphism of the free pro-\(2\) group \(E=\langle\sigma,x\rangle\) sending the two primitive cyclic subgroups generated by \(\sigma\) and \(x^\sigma\sigma x\) to their fifth-power subgroups with common exponent \(5\).

NEXT — Prove the rank-two relative power lemma by lifting the invertible Iwasawa norm solution through the lower \(2\)-central series; the first nontrivial graded correction is already forced and solvable in weight \(4\).

[BREAKTHROUGH]
